IVY HILL POST ACUTE NURSING & REHABILITATION LLC

CCN: 395525 · PHILADELPHIA, PA 19150 · Philadelphia County

Overview

Address
1401 IVY HILL ROAD, PHILADELPHIA, PA 19150
Phone
2152335605
Certified beds
145
Avg daily residents
136 (94% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1982-04-28
Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.72 3.64 3.95 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.50 0.64 0.53 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.86 0.81 0.91 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.37 2.19 2.51 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.36 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.12 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.44
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.40
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.66
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
48.4%
annual
RN turnover
21.4%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.29
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.94
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-03-28 17 17 16 88 1 88
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-06-13 22 11 12 112 1 112
Total weighted health score
94.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (49)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

48 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. E 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. E 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0943 Give their staff education on dementia care, and what abuse, neglect, and exploitation are; and how to report abuse, neglect, and exploitation. E 2025-03-28 2025-05-13
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-12
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-12
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-12
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-12
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-12
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-12
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-12
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-12
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-12
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-12
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2024-06-13 2024-07-12
0568 Properly hold, secure, and manage each resident's personal money which is deposited with the nursing home. D 2023-11-01 2023-11-28
0569 Notify each resident of certain balances and convey resident funds upon discharge, eviction, or death. D 2023-11-01 2023-11-28
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2023-11-01 2023-11-28
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2023-08-17 2023-09-12
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2023-08-17 2023-09-12
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2023-08-17 2023-09-12
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-08-17 2023-09-12
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2023-08-17 2023-09-12
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. E 2023-08-17 2023-09-12
0742 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with mental disorder or psychosocial adjustment difficulty, or who has a history of trauma and/or post-traumatic stress disorder. D 2023-08-17 2023-09-12
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-08-17 2023-09-12
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. D 2023-08-17 2023-09-12
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. D 2023-08-17 2023-09-12
0559 Honor the resident's right to share a room with spouse or roommate of choice and receive written notice before a change is made. E 2023-08-17 2023-09-12
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2023-08-17 2023-09-12
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2023-07-27 2023-08-29
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-07-27 2023-08-29
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. G 2023-04-25 2023-05-16
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2023-04-25 2023-05-16
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-03-01 2023-03-28
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2023-03-01 2023-03-28

Deficiencies 0699 — description Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. 0699.description

Deficiencies 0730 — description Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. 0730.description

Deficiencies 0740 — description Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. 0740.description
Deficiencies 0740 — scope_severity D 0740.scope_severity

Deficiencies 0742 — correction_date 2023-09-12 0742.correction_date
Deficiencies 0742 — description Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with mental disorder or psychosocial adjustment difficulty, or who has a history of trauma and/or post-traumatic stress disorder. 0742.description
Deficiencies 0742 — scope_severity D 0742.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0742 — survey_date 2023-08-17 0742.survey_date
Deficiencies 0742 — tag 0742 0742.tag
Deficiencies 0756 — correction_date 2025-05-13 0756.correction_date
Deficiencies 0756 — description Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. 0756.description
Deficiencies 0756 — scope_severity D 0756.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0756 — survey_date 2025-03-28 0756.survey_date
Deficiencies 0756 — tag 0756 0756.tag
Deficiencies 0758 — correction_date 2025-05-13 0758.correction_date
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — scope_severity D 0758.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0758 — survey_date 2025-03-28 0758.survey_date
Deficiencies 0758 — tag 0758 0758.tag
Deficiencies 0760 — correction_date 2025-05-13 0760.correction_date
Deficiencies 0760 — description Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. 0760.description
Deficiencies 0760 — scope_severity D 0760.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0760 — survey_date 2025-03-28 0760.survey_date
Deficiencies 0760 — tag 0760 0760.tag
Deficiencies 0761 — correction_date 2024-07-12 0761.correction_date
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — scope_severity D 0761.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0761 — survey_date 2024-06-13 0761.survey_date
Deficiencies 0761 — tag 0761 0761.tag
